expectations.text: Output file migration-strategy.pptx exists and is valid
expectations.evidence: Lines 765-769: outputPath = path.join(outputDir, 'migration-strategy.pptx'); await pptx.writeFile({ fileName: outputPath }). The file writes to the correct name using pptxgenjs writeFile.

expectations.text: Uses shadow factory pattern (not shared shadow objects)
expectations.evidence: No shadow factory pattern found anywhere in the file. Grep for 'shadow' and 'Shadow' returns no matches. The build uses an SVG-to-image pipeline where all 3 slides are rendered as full-page SVG images and added to PPTX as images. No pptxgenjs shadow objects are used at all.

expectations.text: No # prefix on pptxgenjs color values (fill, color, line properties)
expectations.evidence: All Carbon tokens include '#' prefix (lines 25-65, e.g., gray100: '#161616', blue60: '#0f62fe'). However, the code uses an SVG capture approach, so these colors flow into SVG attributes (not pptxgenjs properties). The only pptxgenjs color is at line 755: s.background = { color: '161616' } which is correct, but the design approach makes this mostly moot. The palette convention violates the no-# rule.

expectations.text: Uses renderGradientBar for accent bars with h >= 0.08
expectations.evidence: No renderGradientBar function exists. The build uses SVG-to-image rendering where gradient bars are drawn as SVG <rect> elements with linearGradient fills inside full-page SVG compositions. Accent bars are SVG elements at lines 487-491 (height: 8px in SVG coordinate space), not rendered via a reusable renderGradientBar helper.
Lines 749 (await svgToPng) and 769 (await pptx.writeFile) are properly awaited. The build() function at line 729 is async and called with .catch at line 773.

expectations.text: slide.background set to white on content slides
expectations.evidence: Line 755: s.background = { color: '161616' } — all slides use dark gray (Carbon gray100) background, not white. The SVG-to-image approach renders dark-themed slides as full-page images. No white backgrounds are used.
